ai_insights.executive_summary: TNO delivered a network performance score of 59.6 out of 100 (where 50 = network average) in February 2026, reflecting solid operational discipline across the portfolio. Scheduler Compliance stands at 80.0%, marginally above the company average of 79.97%, while Average Delay of 8.9 minutes aligns closely with the company average of 8.92 minutes. The network continues to demonstrate strong iAssign adoption at 98.4% utilization, though two clinics require targeted intervention to address outlier performance patterns.
highlights: Cleveland and Smyrna emerged as standout performers in February, each achieving a Performance Score of 83 and 85 out of 100 respectively (compared to the network average of 50). Cleveland's Average Delay of 3.4 minutes sits substantially below the company average of 8.92 minutes, paired with Chair Utilization of 81.2%, the highest in the portfolio and well above the Onco Network benchmark of 67.8%. Clarksville similarly excels with Scheduler Compliance of 91.0%—a 14.0 percentage point gain above the company average of 79.97%—while maintaining an Average Delay of 7.5 minutes and a Performance Score of 81 out of 100.
concerns: We noticed that Cookeville is experiencing an Average Delay of 22.6 minutes, a 153% gap above the company average of 8.92 minutes and representing the largest outlier in the network; despite solid Scheduler Compliance of 88.0%, the scheduling rhythm does not appear to be translating into timely patient flow, suggesting a potential mismatch between appointment templates and actual treatment duration. Additionally, EPCBC warrants attention across multiple dimensions: Scheduler Compliance of 65.9% falls 14.1 percentage points below the company average of 79.97%, iAssign Utilization stands at 56.0% compared to the network average of 98.4%, and Average Delay of 19.6 minutes sits 120% above the company average of 8.92 minutes.
recommendations: We recommend a focused review of Cookeville's scheduling templates and nurse-to-chair assignment protocols to identify where delays accumulate between appointment slot and treatment start. The 22.6-minute average delay—more than double the company baseline—suggests either overly aggressive scheduling intervals, extended prep workflows, or nurse capacity constraints that warrant direct observation and audit. This targeted operational fix should take priority in March, with weekly tracking of delay reduction milestones.

ai_insights.executive_summary: TNO's March 2026 performance reflects a network in transition: scheduler compliance averaged 89.0% (above the company benchmark of 87.35%), and average patient delay stood at 8.2 minutes (below the company average of 9.07 minutes), yet meaningful variance across clinics signals opportunities for standardization. Eight clinics showed statistically significant month-over-month improvements in overtime metrics, with Cleveland reducing minutes past close per patient by 84.6% (from 27.6 in February to 4.2 in March) and Columbia improving by 63.3% (22.1 to 8.1 minutes), alongside a 56.2% gain at EPCBC in iAssign utilization (56.0% to 87.5%).
highlights: Cleveland and Smyrna emerged as our strongest performers this month. Cleveland achieved a composite performance score of 81 out of 100 (where 50 represents network average), with an average delay of just 2.8 minutes—well below the company average of 9.07 minutes—while maintaining 81.0% chair utilization and near-perfect overtime control (0.2 treatments past closing daily). Smyrna posted a performance score of 89 out of 100, our highest in the network, pairing 94.3% scheduler compliance (7.6 points above the company average of 87.35%) with 87.3% chair utilization, which represents healthy overbooking normalisation toward optimal capacity use.
concerns: We noticed Murfreesboro and Gallatin face escalating overtime pressure. Murfreesboro reports 4.9 treatments running past closing each day—more than four times the company norm of 1.0—despite solid scheduler compliance of 89.0% and strong chair utilization of 74.6%; this suggests a scheduling-to-capacity mismatch worth examining together. EPCBC, though showing encouraging iAssign adoption gains (56.0% to 87.5% month-over-month), continues to face the network's largest delay challenge at 21.2 minutes—134% above the company average of 9.07 minutes—signaling a deeper workflow constraint beyond technology adoption alone.
recommendations: We recommend a focused operational audit at EPCBC to map the patient journey from check-in through treatment start, comparing actual workflow steps against our iOptimize scheduling template recommendations. Given that EPCBC's scheduler compliance sits at 72.3% (15.4 points below the company average of 87.35%) and delays remain at 21.2 minutes despite rising iAssign utilization, the bottleneck likely resides in clinical task sequencing or nurse-to-chair task allocation rather than scheduling discipline—a review of how treatment prep orders align with chair availability would help surface whether we're scheduling to realistic clinical capacity.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
